author: @sharpkidthugga7980

text:
I've had a couple more ideas for covers since this one, but unfortunately I've moved to Linux in the interim and so far as I can tell there's no documented way to get MPC/SMP/GMP working on Linux yet.
I've been meaning to get a dual boot setup going but started having some serious issues with my old hard drive and my new motherboard is too new for Windows 7 to work on and it's all just a mess.
author: @MediaMunkee

text: I was just thinking the other day about how fun it would be to cover this song with GMP, what with the extended range + sustain. Sustain makes the swan absolutely perfect for this; I mean, it's the exact same sample used in ActRaiser to begin with, sustain just makes it sound even closer to the OST. This is pretty damn good stuff here. Think I've got a spot in the favorites playlist for this one :P
author: @DeepFreeze7575

text: Sometimes I really wonder what the origins are for the original SNES samples. The flower sounds to me like it's also used in EarthBound but I've never actually bothered to check. But yeah it's great being able to use the swan sample in a lower octave, I often feel it's kind of squandered as an instrument just for being an octave or two too high for most practical use.
author: @MediaMunkee

text:
The flower's trumpet sample is in several games, iirc, including EarthBound and Donkey Kong Country. I know a couple of others as well: the plane's guitar sample is in EarthBound, while the car's organ sample is in Super Castlevania IV.

It is tough, finding ways to use the swan. I know I mainly relegate it to typical orchestra hit duties, but I do find occasional use for it in just making things stand out a little more. The sharp sound of an orchestra hit can sometimes provide just enough volume to make a background element stand out a little better.
author: @DeepFreeze7575
